description: Grasses and other herbaceous plants cover the open land called 'pelowa'. A meadow may be grazed, cut for hay, deliberately maintained, or left to seasonal growth. 'kosha' (field) centres the land's continuing use instead.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into middle-height front 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and let the voice pass around its sides into stressed middle-height rounded 'o'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for the 'w' glide, then open into central 'a'.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A meadow can support food plants and pollinators. Its tending can also make room for grazing, play, or habitat. The word leaves those uses and ecological results open for the local account.

semantic_domains.nature: open land dominated by grasses and other non-woody plants
semantic_domains.spatial: a recognisable opening within cultivated or wilder land
